name: Registry Discovery CTA Grid
description: Editorial quick-start discovery cards for the public registry homepage. Each card helps builders choose a path into the registry with a strong icon, concise framing copy, and a clearly-owned Decantr action treatment.

editorial.description: Three discovery cards in a responsive grid. Each card has an icon badge, eyebrow, title, supporting description, and a bottom action rail that reads like the next step a builder should take.

slots.card-icon: Compact icon badge tied to the discovery path. Distinct but not louder than the title.
slots.card-eyebrow: Uppercase label using d-label treatment to explain the decision lens for the card.
slots.card-title: Strong section heading that frames the user intent.
slots.card-description: Two-to-four lines of editorial helper copy that explains why a builder would take this path.
slots.card-action: Primary CTA rail with icon, label, and supporting hint. The action must feel clearly clickable, not like body copy.

visual_brief: A premium discovery grid that feels closer to a product decision surface than a checklist. Cards should be calm, dark, and atmospheric with subtle accent gradients, a strong top-left icon moment, and a bottom CTA rail that is unmistakably interactive. The CTA should not collapse into generic ghost-button text. Think curated launchpad cards: crisp type, clear action hierarchy, and just enough motion and accent contrast to feel current without becoming noisy.

responsive.mobile: Single-column cards with full-width CTA rails. The icon, eyebrow, title, description, and CTA stack vertically with no dead space.
responsive.tablet: Two-column layout when space allows. Cards keep even height and a clear action zone at the bottom.
responsive.desktop: Three-column editorial grid with balanced card height, stronger hover treatment, and CTA rails aligned along the bottom edge.

motion.micro: Discovery cards may lift slightly and brighten their accent treatment on hover. CTA rails should respond clearly on hover and focus with a directional sense of momentum.
motion.transitions: Grid cards may reveal with a short stagger or fade-up sequence, but the surface should still feel curated rather than animated for its own sake.

layout_hints.card_structure: Use one visual lane per card: icon + eyebrow at the top, narrative in the middle, action rail at the bottom. Avoid chip clouds, diagnostics, or inline metadata.
layout_hints.cta_treatment: CTA rails should be explicit actions with iconography and supporting hint copy. Prefer directional arrow or external-link affordances over plain text-only links.
layout_hints.tone_mapping: Each discovery card can use its own accent family so the user can quickly parse blueprint-first vs showcase-first vs registry-wide exploration.

keyboard: Tab moves through discovery CTAs in card order.
announcements: Card title, supporting narrative, and CTA intent should be available without relying on color or hover effects.
accessibility.focus_management: Each discovery card should expose a clear focusable CTA rather than forcing keyboard users to parse the whole card surface as one unlabeled block.

composition.DiscoveryCard: Card(d-surface, raised) > [CardHead > [IconBadge + Eyebrow] + Title + Description + ActionRail]
composition.ActionRail: Button(d-interactive, primary or strong secondary) > [ActionLabel + ActionHint + TrailingIcon]
